sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports experiencing persistent, sharp chest pain radiating to his left arm, which began approximately 2 hours ago. He states the pain is rated 8/10 and is not relieved by over-the-counter analgesics. He denies shortness of breath or palpitations. He also reports feeling nauseous.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 140/90 mmHg. Heart rate is 88 bpm and regular. Oxygen saturation is 98% on room air. Auscultation reveals no murmurs. The lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient is presenting with acute chest pain, concerning for possible myocardial infarction.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Activate EMS immediately. Obtain 12-lead ECG. Administer aspirin 325 mg. Start oxygen at 2L via nasal cannula. Obtain cardiac enzymes (Troponin, CK-MB).
